name: bootstrap
description: VPS provisioning, HA cluster setup, front door security, ingress, MetalLB, secrets bootstrapping, GitHub CI.
sprintGoal: A complete bootstrap story: any developer can clone the repo, fill in config.yaml, run bootstrap.sh, and have a production-grade 3-node HA Kubernetes cluster with GitHub CI validating every subsequent PR.

stories.title: Scaffold repo structure and global Helm values
stories.description: Create the complete directory structure outlined in CLAUDE.md. Create a global values file at charts/_globals/values.yaml with domain, storageClass, and keycloak placeholders. Create a config.yaml.example that users fill in before running bootstrap. Create a minimal README.md explaining the project and prerequisites.
acceptanceCriteria: All directories from CLAUDE.md repo structure exist
charts/_globals/values.yaml exists with global.domain, global.storageClass, global.keycloak stubs
config.yaml.example exists with comments explaining every field
README.md explains what Sovereign is, prerequisites, and links to docs/quickstart.md
shellcheck passes on any .sh files created
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Well-scoped scaffolding story. ACs are file-existence checks which are binary and verifiable.

stories.title: Bootstrap script: generic VPS and Hetzner provisioning
stories.description: Create bootstrap/bootstrap.sh as the main entry point that reads config.yaml and calls the appropriate provider script. Create bootstrap/providers/hetzner.sh using the Hetzner Cloud CLI (hcloud) to provision a CX21 or larger server, install K3s, and output kubeconfig. Create bootstrap/providers/generic-vps.sh that SSH-installs K3s on any Ubuntu 22.04 server given an IP and SSH key. Both must print estimated cost and Cloudflare wildcard DNS instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/bootstrap.sh reads config.yaml and routes to correct provider
bootstrap/providers/hetzner.sh provisions server via hcloud CLI and outputs kubeconfig
bootstrap/providers/generic-vps.sh SSH-installs K3s on any Ubuntu 22.04 server
Both scripts print monthly cost estimate and Cloudflare DNS setup instructions
shellcheck passes on all .sh files
bootstrap/verify.sh checks that kubectl can connect and nodes are Ready
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Clear provider scripts with binary shellcheck and file-existence ACs. Achievable 4 because SSH-based K3s install has external dependencies.

stories.title: Bootstrap scripts: AWS EC2 and DigitalOcean
stories.description: Create bootstrap/providers/aws-ec2.sh using the AWS CLI to provision a t3.small (t3.small minimum) or t3.small, install K3s, output kubeconfig and cost estimate. Create bootstrap/providers/digitalocean.sh using doctl to provision the cheapest Droplet ($4-6/mo), install K3s, output kubeconfig. Create docs/providers/hetzner.md, docs/providers/aws-ec2-free-tier.md, docs/providers/digitalocean.md with step-by-step human instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/providers/aws-ec2.sh provisions EC2 instance and installs K3s
bootstrap/providers/digitalocean.sh provisions Droplet and installs K3s
Both scripts print cost estimates and DNS instructions
All three provider docs exist with prerequisites, steps, and copy-pasteable commands
shellcheck passes on all .sh files
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Two provider scripts plus docs. ACs are binary file/shellcheck checks. Achievable 4 due to cloud API dependencies.

stories.title: Helm chart: Cilium CNI with network policies
stories.description: Create charts/cilium/ as a Helm chart that wraps the official Cilium chart with opinionated defaults: deny-all default network policy, Hubble UI enabled, WireGuard encryption between nodes. Create argocd-apps/platform/cilium-app.yaml. Cilium must use global.domain for any ingress (Hubble UI).
acceptanceCriteria: charts/cilium/Chart.yaml, values.yaml, templates/ exist
Default network policy is deny-all with explicit allow for DNS and same-namespace
Hubble UI ingress uses {{ .Values.global.domain }}
helm lint passes
helm template | kubectl apply --dry-run passes
argocd-apps/platform/cilium-app.yaml exists and is valid
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Clear Helm chart story with runnable verification commands. Achievable 4: Cilium chart dependency download required.

stories.title: ArgoCD root App-of-Apps setup
stories.description: Create charts/argocd/ wrapping argo/argo-cd with opinionated values: SSO stub for Keycloak OIDC (enabled after Phase 2), repo server with the sovereign repo pre-configured, admin password from Sealed Secret. Create argocd-apps/root-app.yaml that watches the argocd-apps/ directory and creates all child apps. Create argocd-apps/platform/, argocd-apps/security/, argocd-apps/observability/, argocd-apps/devex/, argocd-apps/testing/ directories with placeholder README files.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/argocd/ exists with Keycloak OIDC stub in values.yaml (disabled by default)
argocd-apps/root-app.yaml points to the sovereign repo argocd-apps/ directory
All 5 tier subdirectories exist in argocd-apps/ with README.md
helm lint passes
Bootstrap script includes step to install ArgoCD and apply root-app.yaml
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: App-of-Apps pattern is well understood. ACs are concrete file-existence and helm-lint checks.

stories.title: GitHub CI: comprehensive PR validation (HA gate, lint, vendor audit, bootstrap check)
stories.description:
Create the GitHub Actions CI pipeline that validates every PR to the sovereign repo. This must land at priority 8 — before any further chart work — so all subsequent stories benefit from automated validation. Two workflows:

.github/workflows/validate.yml — runs on every PR and push to main. Five parallel jobs:

Job 1 helm-validate (matrix over every chart in charts/):
helm lint charts/<name>/; helm template charts/<name>/ | kubectl apply --dry-run=client; assert rendered output contains PodDisruptionBudget (fail if missing); assert rendered output contains podAntiAffinity in every Deployment/StatefulSet (fail if missing); assert values.yaml replicaCount >= 2 (fail if 1 or absent); assert no image tag ':latest' anywhere in values.yaml; assert image.repository references global.imageRegistry (not a hardcoded registry).

Job 2 shell-validate: shellcheck on all bootstrap/**/*.sh vendor/*.sh vendor/recipes/*/build.sh; assert every vendor/*.sh handles --dry-run (grep DRY_RUN or dry.run); assert every vendor/*.sh handles --backup.

Job 3 vendor-audit (only runs if vendor/ files changed): run vendor/audit.sh — exits non-zero on license violations; validate VENDORS.yaml schema (every entry has all required fields); validate every recipe.yaml has rollout.strategy, rollout.max_unavailable, backup.priority.

Job 4 argocd-validate: kubectl apply --dry-run=client on all argocd-apps/**/*.yaml; assert every Application has revisionHistoryLimit: 3.

Job 5 bootstrap-validate: shellcheck bootstrap/providers/*.sh; grep bootstrap/bootstrap.sh for node count guard (nodes.count < 3 check must be present); grep each provider script to assert it contains a loop over node count; grep to assert no provider script references a single hard-coded server IP.

.github/workflows/release.yml — on tag push vX.Y.Z: run validate.yml first; package all Helm charts with helm package; publish to GitHub Pages as a Helm repository with helm repo index; create GitHub Release with auto-generated changelog. CI must complete under 5 minutes. Cache helm/kubectl/shellcheck binaries between runs.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/validate.yml triggers on PR and push to main with 5 parallel jobs
helm-validate fails if any chart is missing PodDisruptionBudget in rendered output
helm-validate fails if any chart has replicaCount default < 2
helm-validate fails if any values.yaml contains :latest image tag
helm-validate fails if image.repository does not use global.imageRegistry
shell-validate runs shellcheck on all .sh files and fails on any error
shell-validate fails if any vendor/*.sh lacks --dry-run and --backup handling
vendor-audit runs vendor/audit.sh and fails on license violations
vendor-audit validates recipe.yaml rollout and backup sections are present
argocd-validate dry-runs all ArgoCD Application manifests
argocd-validate fails if any Application lacks revisionHistoryLimit: 3
bootstrap-validate fails if node count guard is absent from bootstrap.sh
.github/workflows/release.yml publishes Helm charts to GitHub Pages on tag
Full CI run completes in under 5 minutes
Each job fails independently with a clear, actionable error message
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Highly detailed with exact job names, assertions, and a 5-minute SLA. Achievable 4: GitHub Actions YAML can be verbose but the logic is clear.
